what_it_does: This file is a maximal-coverage descriptive map of AREA C (ops, foundation, orienteering, docs) for the Company substrate. It inventories the service registry, systemd units, VRAM loadouts, runtime architecture, knowledge layer, external terrain ledger, documentation conventions, and open questions as of 2026-06-28.
observations: services.json is treated as the single self-describing source of truth for 45 services across groups core/brain/voice/models/reach/jobs.
No service auto-starts at boot except the three core surface services (canvas, bridge, remote); everything else is on-demand via `company up`.
Loadouts are expressed as named combos in services.json, with variant support via `extends` and `swap`.
All brain chat models use vLLM with prefix caching, auto tool choice, and a patched no-think chat template.
embed-pplx is served by custom Python (serve_pplx_embed.py), not vLLM, because its architecture is not in vLLM 0.21.
rerank-jina was moved to GPU on 2026-06-28, dropping warm latency from ~16s to ~0.05s.
Port 8004 is claimed by both embed-jina-v5 and embed-qwen3, an unresolved conflict.
tts-kokoro has an empty/unverified model-id in services.json and its weights location is not confirmed.
chat-nemotron at ~16.6GB is essentially a solo-only model on the 16GB RTX 4080 ceiling.
The voice-venvs external directory is 48GB, one of the largest tracked terrain entries.
TIM.md is described as a living, additive foundation document with 11 major sections and a verbatim exchanges archive.
The operating model explicitly states zero human developers; all engineering is AI-operated across sessions.
The orienteering ledger is framed as a map of what-is, not a blueprint of what-should-be.
Foundation/ is described as irreplaceable because its synthesis would have to be re-derived from transcripts.
Session supervisor is restricted to 127.0.0.1 as an exposure boundary.
Ollama is a system-managed unit separate from the user-unit `company` CLI lifecycle.
standouts: services.json as the single source of truth for the entire runtime landscape.
VRAM-first design: combos, swaps, and co-resident constraints are load-bearing.
No-human-operators principle shaping memory, documentation, and tooling.
Custom non-vLLM embedder (embed-pplx) explicitly surfaced and verified live.
Port 8004 collision and tts-kokoro model-id gap flagged as unresolved.
conventions: Frontmatter with type, area, register, date, coverage, and typed relates-to links.
Aliases in wiki-style links such as [[services.json]] and [[orienteering/INDEX]].
Coverage metadata claiming scanned: all, depth: maximal-coverage, last_read date.
Tables for services, ports, VRAM, files, and critical paths.
File-line citations and dated verification notes.
concerns: Port 8004 collision between embed-jina-v5 and embed-qwen3 is unresolved and could prevent both from running.
tts-kokoro model-id is empty/unverified, raising operational uncertainty.
The claim of 18 user units + 1 system unit is hard to reconcile with the longer enumerated list in section 1.9.
The phrase 'no auto-start at boot' coexists with 'autostart=true only for surface layer' without fully explaining the distinction.
Coverage says 'scanned: all' and 'maximal-coverage' while also listing open questions, which is honest but borders on completeness overclaim.
notes: A dense, dated inventory of the Company's operational and foundational substrate, meant to orient future AI agents without re-deriving the landscape.
questions: Which service should be reassigned from port 8004, and to which port?
What is the actual on-disk model-id for tts-kokoro?
How exactly does 'autostart=true for surface layer' interact with the 'no auto-start at boot' decision?
What is the commander's-bridge interface mentioned as not yet designed?
What mechanism will enforce the no-repeat KPI for correction propagation?
What are RHM/modes and how will they become a Company primitive?
When and how should spiral-review of TIM.md be triggered?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a comprehensive AREA C inventory/map; actual content matches, adding operational synthesis, design rationale, and unresolved issues.
apparent_themes: Resource constraints as first-class design input
intention_signals: To produce an authoritative, AI-readable terrain map of the ops/foundation layer so future agents can orient quickly, avoid re-deriving the substrate, and see both locked decisions and open threads.
novelty: It treats a personal AI runtime as a formal ledger with descriptive register, VRAM-budget loadouts, combo/swap semantics, and an explicit 'no humans' operating model.
contribution: A structured, dated inventory that records the service registry, runtime architecture, resource budgets, external terrain, knowledge-layer conventions, and unresolved issues—serving as an orientation artifact for future agents.
summary_for_embedding: A maximal-coverage descriptive map of AREA C cataloging the Company's ops service registry, systemd units, VRAM loadouts, foundation knowledge layer, orienteering terrain ledger, docs conventions, and unresolved issues as of 2026-06-28.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single authoritative orientation document for AI agents maintaining or extending the Company's operational and foundational substrate.
